prompt:
I own the intake side of the facility event ledger: badge readers and asset scanners upload free-text fields (door names, asset labels, operator notes) and we flatten each event onto ONE line for the site controller. Right now anything with a tab, a newline, or a stray byte from the older Latin-1 scanners corrupts the line framing. The controller vendor finally published their quoting rules, so I need a proper Quote/Unquote pair in a fresh package `fieldquote`. The acceptance tests are already in fieldquote_test.go (don't touch that file, or go.mod); write fieldquote.go so `go test -race ./...` passes. Stdlib only. And no, wrapping strconv doesn't satisfy the vendor spec — their hex casing and control-character rules differ from Go's quoting, the tests will tell on you.

The vendor rules, as I read them:

Quote(s string) string — wrap in double quotes and escape so the result is one line of printable ASCII plus any verbatim printable non-ASCII:
- `"` becomes `\"`, `\` becomes `\\`; newline, tab, carriage return become `\n`, `\t`, `\r`.
- Every OTHER non-printable rune below 0x80 becomes `\xHH` — two UPPERCASE hex digits, no named escapes (so bell is `\x07`, vertical tab is `\x0B`, DEL is `\x7F`).
- Bytes that aren't valid UTF-8 also become `\xHH` (uppercase), one escape per raw byte.
- Printable runes pass through verbatim, including non-ASCII (accents, emoji).
- Non-printable runes at or above 0x80 become `\uHHHH` (4 uppercase hex digits) up to U+FFFF, else `\UHHHHHHHH` (8 uppercase hex digits).

Printable(r rune) bool — the classification Quote uses, exported because the report generator wants it too: ASCII 0x20..0x7E is printable; above 0x7F defer to unicode.IsPrint; everything else (all other controls, and every space-ish rune except plain space) is not.

Unquote(q string) (string, error) — strict inverse for the reading side: q must start with `"` and end with the matching unescaped `"` with nothing after it. Recognized escapes: `\"`, `\\`, `\n`, `\t`, `\r`, `\xHH` (appends the RAW BYTE — two `\x` escapes may combine into one UTF-8 character, and a lone high byte is kept as-is), `\uHHHH` and `\UHHHHHHHH` (append that code point encoded as UTF-8). Hex digits are accepted in either case on input. Errors are distinguished by exported sentinel values usable with errors.Is:
- ErrNotQuoted — missing/unbalanced enclosing quotes, an interior unescaped quote before the end, or trailing content after the closing quote;
- ErrTrailingBackslash — the input ends right after a backslash;
- ErrUnknownEscape — a backslash followed by anything not listed above;
- ErrBadHex — a hex escape cut short or containing a non-hex digit;
- ErrBadRune — `\u`/`\U` naming a surrogate (U+D800..U+DFFF, surrogate PAIRS included — we are not JSON) or a value above U+10FFFF;
- ErrBareControl — a raw unescaped control rune below 0x20 inside the value.

Round trip is the contract: Unquote(Quote(s)) == s for anything, including invalid UTF-8. Table-driven tests pin all of this — read them as the spec where my wording is loose.
